zh.lifeSummary: 當Amy de Wythe於1390年出生在英國諾福爾可伯納姆 索普時，她的父親Sir John Wythe Withe20歲，母親Sibilla St Omer16歲。她於大約1403年與Sir John Calthorpe結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1451年在她的家鄉享年61歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Amy de Wythe于1390年出生在英国諾福爾可伯納姆 索普时，她的父亲Sir John Wythe Withe20岁，母亲Sibilla St Omer16岁。她于大约1403年与Sir John Calthorpe结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1451年在她的家乡享年61岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Wythe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Suffolk): topographic name for someone who lived by a willow tree or in an area where willow trees grew, from Middle English wythe (Old English withthe). Alternatively, a variant of White or Wight .

History: American bearers of the surname Wythe trace their ancestry to Thomas Wythe, who emigrated from England to VA in 1680. One of his descendants was the statesman and jurist George Wythe (1726–1806), mentor of Thomas Jefferson and one of the signers of the Declaration of Independence.

variants.name: White
meanings: English: from Middle English white, wit (Old English hwīt ‘white’), hence a nickname for someone with white hair or a pale complexion. In some cases it is perhaps from a Middle English survival of the Old English personal name Hwīta, a short form of names in Hwīt- (from hwīt ‘white’). The name may also be topographic, referring to someone who lived by a bend or curve in a river or road (from Old English wiht ‘bend’), the source of the placename of Great Whyte in Ramsey, Huntingdonshire (compare Wight ). This name is also a variant of Wight . The surname White is also very common among African Americans.

Irish and Scottish: adopted for any of several Irish and Scottish Gaelic names based on bán ‘white, fair’ (see Bain 1, McElwain ) or fionn ‘fair’ (see Finn 1). The English surname has been Gaelicized in Ireland as de Faoite.

Americanized form (translation into English) of various European surnames meaning ‘white’, for example German Weiss , French Blanc , Polish Białas (see Bialas ), Slovenian Belec , or any other synonymous Slavic surname beginning with Bel-, Bev-, Biel- or Bil-.

variants.name: With
meanings: German: from a short form of a Slavic personal name based on the Old Slavic element vit ‘mighty, powerful’ (see Wit ).

nameMeanings.name: Amy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Anglicized form of Old French Amee ‘beloved’. This originated in part as a vernacular nickname, in part as a form of Latin Amata. The latter is ostensibly the feminine form of the past participle of amare ‘to love’, but in fact it may have had a different, pre-Roman, origin; it was borne in classical mythology by the wife of King Latinus , whose daughter Lavinia married Aeneas and (according to the story in the Aeneid) became the mother of the Roman people.

variants.name: Aimie
meanings: Anglicized form of Old French Amee ‘beloved’. This originated in part as a vernacular nickname, in part as a form of Latin Amata. The latter is ostensibly the feminine form of the past participle of amare ‘to love’, but in fact it may have had a different, pre-Roman, origin; it was borne in classical mythology by the wife of King Latinus , whose daughter Lavinia married Aeneas and (according to the story in the Aeneid) became the mother of the Roman people.

variants.name: Aimée
meanings: originally a vernacular nickname meaning ‘beloved’, from the past participle of French aimer ‘to love’ (Latin amare; compare Amy ). It has been in use in French since the Middle Ages, although it has never been very common. It is now also used, with or without the accent, as a given name in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Amie
meanings: Variant spelling of   Amy  , corresponding in form to the French vocabulary word meaning ‘(female) friend’.

variants.name: Ami
meanings: Anglicized form of Old French Amee ‘beloved’. This originated in part as a vernacular nickname, in part as a form of Latin Amata. The latter is ostensibly the feminine form of the past participle of amare ‘to love’, but in fact it may have had a different, pre-Roman, origin; it was borne in classical mythology by the wife of King Latinus , whose daughter Lavinia married Aeneas and (according to the story in the Aeneid) became the mother of the Roman people.

en.lifeSummary: When Amy de Wythe was born in 1390, in Burnham Thorpe, Norfolk, England, her father, Sir John Wythe Withe, was 20 and her mother, Sibilla St Omer, was 16. She married Sir John Calthorpe about 1403. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 5 daughters. She died in 1451, in her hometown, at the age of 61.

ko.lifeSummary: Amy de Wythe(이)가 1390년 영국 노르폴크 번함 소에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John Wythe Withe(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Sibilla St Omer(은)는 16세였다. 그녀는 대략 1403년 Sir John Calthorpe(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1451년 그녀의 고향에서 61세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Amy de Wythe родилась в 1390 г. в местности бурнам таурп, норфолк, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir John Wythe Withe было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Sibilla St Omer, 16. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John Calthorpe около 1403 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 5 дочерей. Она умерла в 1451 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 61 год.
